meaning: n.謌ｷ螟門耳謌ｿ,闊ｹ荳雁耳謌ｿ
examples: We've been told to sell poppies to airmen in their cookhouse. I don't have the courage: I'm far too shy!
He was promoted to company quartermaster from a cookhouse soldier rank by rank.
In 1954, in the cookhouse of a logging and sawmill settlement in northern New Hampshire, an anxious twelve-year-old boy mistakes the local constable's girlfriend for a bear.
Each group had its own cookhouse tent and its own toilet truck.
Back at the cookhouse, Caton hands me a catalog for St.
